helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function ssim at 0x7fa9eb1f8f70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

kornia/test_metrics.py:318: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.metrics.endpoint_error.AEPE'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function get_laf_center at 0x7f724f4d5c60>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function set_laf_orientation at 0x7f724f4d5e10>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function laf_to_three_points at 0x7f724f4d6560>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.feature.mkd.MKDDescriptor'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.clahe.RandomClahe'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.gaussian_illumination.RandomGaussianIllumination'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.jpeg.RandomJPEG'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.planckian_jitter.RandomPlanckianJitter'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.random_rain.RandomRain'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.random_snow.RandomSnow'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.geometric.crop.RandomCrop'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.geometric.perspective.RandomPerspective'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.geometric.vertical_flip.RandomVerticalFlip'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.mix.transplantation.RandomTransplantation'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._3d.geometric.horizontal_flip.RandomHorizontalFlip3D'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._3d.mix.transplantation.RandomTransplantation3D'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.geometric.resize.SmallestMaxSize'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/test_feature4.py:23: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.feature.integrated.SIFTFeature'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.feature.integrated.SIFTFeatureScaleSpace'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.

        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.vector.Vector2'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function conv_quad_interp3d at 0x7f92de0108b0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function spatial_soft_argmax2d at 0x7f92de010820>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        trace_kwargs,
        test_args,
        test_kwargs,

        backend_compile,
        tolerance=1e-3,
        deterministic=True,

            pytest.skip()

object = <function nms3d at 0x7f92ddffbe20>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function find_essential at 0x7f9d580a0040>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function decompose_essential_matrix at 0x7f9d5808bc70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function relative_camera_motion at 0x7f9d5808bf40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function fundamental_from_projections at 0x7f9d580a08b0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.

            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

object = <function symmetrical_epipolar_distance at 0x7f9d5808a4d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246:

object = <function projection_from_KRt at 0x7f9d5808b6d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function scale_intrinsics at 0x7f9d5808b640>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.liegroup.so3.So3'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function find_homography_dlt at 0x7fef185acf70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function find_homography_lines_dlt at 0x7fef185ad1b0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function rad2deg at 0x7fdb7b50fac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function angle_to_rotation_matrix at 0x7fdb7aef5510>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function convert_affinematrix_to_homography at 0x7fdb7aef4700>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function normalize_pixel_coordinates3d at 0x7fdb7aef53f0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function quaternion_to_rotation_matrix at 0x7fdb7aef4ca0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function normalize_quaternion at 0x7fdb7aef4c10>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source
